person.name: John Stewart Laird of Lorn

memoryArtifacts.headline: Castle Stalker
memoryArtifacts.description:
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia


Castle Stalker with the Lynn of Lorne and Lismore beyond
Geographical location of Castle Stalker

Castle Stalker (Scottish Gaelic: Caisteal an Stalcaire) is a four-storey tower house or keep picturesquely set on a tidal islet on Loch Laich, an inlet off Loch Linnhe. It is about 1.5 miles (2.4 km) north-east of Port Appin, Argyll, Scotland, and is visible from the A828 road about midway between Oban and Glen Coe. The islet is accessible (with difficulty) from the shore at low tide. The name "Stalker" comes from the Gaelic Stalcaire, meaning "hunter" or "falconer". The island castle is one of the best-preserved medieval tower-houses to survive in western Scotland[1] and is a Category A listed building.[2] It stands in the Lynn of Lorn National Scenic Area, one of forty such areas in Scotland. Around 1388 the Stewarts took over the Lordship of Lorn, and it is believed that they built the castle in its present form around the 1440s.

zh.lifeSummary: 當John Stewart Laird of Lorn於1350年出生在蘇格蘭培爾培斯時，他的父親Sir Robert Stewart of Shambothy, Innermeath21歲，母親Heiress of Holkettle Margaret Bethune - heiress of holkettle22歲。他於1374年與Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne在蘇格蘭阿吉爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1421年4月26日在蘇格蘭阿吉爾享年71歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭費伏洛斯。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When John Stewart Laird of Lorn was born in 1350, in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, his father, Sir Robert Stewart of Shambothy, Innermeath, was 21 and his mother, Heiress of Holkettle Margaret Bethune - heiress of holkettle, was 22. He married Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne in 1374, in Argyll, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 26 April 1421, in Argyll, Scotland, at the age of 71, and was buried in Culross, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne en 1374, à Argyll, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 26 avril 1421, à Argyll, Écosse, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterré à Culross, Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: John Stewart Laird of Lornが1350年にスコットランドペルスシュアイアペルスで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Stewart of Shambothy, Innermeathは21歳，母Heiress of Holkettle Margaret Bethune - heiress of holkettleは22歳であった。1374年にスコットランドアルグイルでIsabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1421年4月26日にスコットランドアルグイルで71歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドフィフクウルロスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Stewart Laird of Lorn(이)가 1350년 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 퍼스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert Stewart of Shambothy, Innermeath(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Heiress of Holkettle Margaret Bethune - heiress of holkettle(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1374년 스컷랜드 아그이에서 Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1421년 4월 26일 스컷랜드 아그이에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 컬로스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Stewart Laird of Lorn родился в 1350 г. в местности пирт, пиртшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir Robert Stewart of Shambothy, Innermeath было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Heiress of Holkettle Margaret Bethune - heiress of holkettle, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne в 1374 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер 26 апреля 1421 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности кулросс, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания.
